name: Zwieback
recipeIngredient: 1/2 tsp ground anise or anise flavoring
recipeInstructions:
In honor of babies and people with knapsacks here is zwieback. You will save a lot of money by making your own.
Preheat oven to 400 degrees F. Scald the milk until lukewarm, then add it to the yeast mixture. Combine the sugar, butter, salt, anise and eggs (beaten), and enough flour to form a dough. Allow the dough to rise until doubled in size. Shape into 3 inch oblong rolls and place close together in a greased pan, spacing them about two inches apart. Let rise again for another 20 minutes. Bake for 20 minutes. When cooled, cut into half-inch slices and brown evenly in the oven. If desired, you can glaze with confectioner's sugar or dust with powdered sugar.
